title: MA2H Inbound Directive (v0.4)
description: Hub->Agent human-initiated message (spec §13). A Hub-attested `from` (human:<id>) addresses `to` (agent:<id>); the agent drains it from its mailbox (§8.7) and verifies the detached signature (§9.7). This is the DELIVERED form: `id` and `from` are Hub-assigned/attested, never agent- or request-supplied. Single type in v0.4: `directive` (the notify/instruction equivalent). No `request`/`action` (human->agent ask/task is deferred) and no `state` (not an agent-owned resume blob).

id.description: Hub-assigned canonical directive id. The agent's at-most-once dedup key.

from.description: Hub-attested author (never request-supplied). `human:<id>` in v0.4; `system:<id>` reserved for Hub-originated directives.

to.description: Addressed agent (`agent:<id>`); the mailbox routing key.

body.description: Human-authored Markdown detail. UNTRUSTED agent input (the inbound mirror of §9.6): a directive drives agent behavior, so treat it as a prompt-/command-injection surface until the signature is verified.

expires_at.description: RFC 3339. After this the Hub stops delivering and drops the directive (no response leg). MUST be in the future at author time.

sensitive.description: Message-level sensitivity; excludes value(s) from export/telemetry.
